negotiation_summary: England opened broadly to all powers offering conditional non-aggression and coordination. With France: mutual stand-down offers — England asked France to refrain from attacking BEL/PIC and to allow peaceful treatment of Brest/Marseille if opportunity arises; France asked England not to attack BRE/PIC and to avoid convoys into France. With Germany: tacit neutrality in Low Countries proposed — England will not push KIE/HOL if Germany holds, and asked Germany not to support French moves into BEL/PIC. With Russia: agreed to avoid northern clashes (England will not move NWY→STP or SWE→FIN) in exchange for Russia not contesting NOR/STP against English northern supply network. With Italy: offered non-interference in the Mediterranean if Italy avoids supporting France vs England in Iberia. With Austria: proposed mutual non-aggression (England will not attack southern centers this phase) and requested Austria avoid contesting future English moves into Low Countries. All offers were framed with the forecast implication that France is more likely to act aggressively (~60% aggression), and England requested explicit commitments where possible. No formal written multi-party treaties were signed yet; responses were conversational and conditional. England signaled it will keep all units active and avoid passive holds.
intent: For S1915M England intends to maintain western consolidation and keep all units active. Specific intended behaviors to reflect negotiated commitments: (1) Do not order attacks into Brest, Paris or Picardy; avoid convoys into French home centers this phase — I intend to honor the France stand-down if France explicitly commits similarly. (2) Hold/offensive posture in the Low Countries only defensively: maintain A HOL and A BEL with supportive/contested moves rather than aggressive pushes into KIE/MUN. (3) Northern posture: no NWY→STP and no SWE→FIN; keep A NWY/A SWE/A DEN positioned to threaten but not provoke Russia per our talk. (4) Mediterranean: do not contest NAP/TUN zones against Italy if they refrain from supporting France against Spain/Portugal. (5) Balkans: no moves into SER/GR E; refrain from opportunistic pushes against Austria this phase. I intend to fulfill these commitments conditionally — if counterparts reciprocate as discussed (notably France and Russia). If clear aggressive moves against our held SCs occur, I will respond aggressively rather than hold. I will ensure all English units receive active orders consistent with these intents.
goals: Maintain and consolidate current 10 supply centers
Avoid a western war with France while preserving naval supremacy
Keep all units active and avoid passive holds this phase
Exploit opportunities to expand into the Low Countries when safe (esp. if France weakens)
Coordinate tacitly with Russia/others to check Turkey/Austria if it creates openings

storyworld_implications.implication: The provided forecast indicates FRANCE has a 60% likelihood of acting aggressively next phase. This increases the risk of French moves into Picardy/Belgium or naval contests; therefore England must prioritize commitments that reduce accidental escalation while retaining freedom to respond if aggression occurs.

forecasting_rationale: The 60% aggression forecast shaped a defensive-but-flexible posture: propose explicit non-aggression promises to France (and request reciprocal commitments) to lower immediate clash risk, avoid convoys/invasions of French homes to signal restraint, and keep northern and Low Country units positioned to counter or exploit French aggression. Conditionality was emphasized: England will honor stand-downs if reciprocated, but will respond aggressively to unreciprocated attacks. This balances the forecasted elevated aggression probability with England's expansionist goal.

negotiation_summary: France initiated multiple short non-aggression / limited-cooperation proposals to England, Germany, Italy, Austria, and Russia. England publicly offered a westward stand-down in exchange for France not attacking BEL/PIC and allowing peaceful seizure of BRE/MAR if dislodgement becomes possible; England emphasized it will keep western forces from attacking France if France commits. England and France both referenced the forecast artifact (60% aggression) as justification for avoiding mutual escalation. Germany was offered a mutual non-aggression in the west (no attack on PAR/BUR if France refrains from supporting England into BEL/HOL). Italy was asked for a no-move pledge on MAR/PIE. Austria was asked to remain focused in the Balkans (no westward pressure) in exchange for France avoiding Trieste/Vienna. Russia was given a note that France will not contest the central Med and asked to avoid allowing an Austrian collapse to Turkey. Outcomes/agreements: England — tentative mutual stand-down if France explicitly commits to not attacking BEL/PIC and to not convoy into France; France publicly committed to seek such stand-downs but will only formally 'Agree' if England confirms no attack on Brest/Paris (mutuality); Germany — proposed non-aggression, no firm reply yet; Italy — requested pledge, no firm reply yet; Austria — requested neutrality westward, no firm reply yet; Russia — informational only. No binding written confirmations received from other powers in this round except England's public proposal. All interactions explicitly referenced the STORYWORLD_FORECAST_ARTIFACT and its 60% aggression implication, which both sides used to justify cautious, reciprocal commitments.
intent: I will pursue aggressive, proactive orders consistent with both seeking survival and honoring explicit pledges where made. Specific intents for S1915M orders: 1) A PAR -> BUR (attack Burgundy) to contest central front and pressure Germany/Belgium area. 2) F BRE -> ENG Channel (or North Sea via Calais?) — more precisely: F BRE -> MAO is not legal; instead I will order F BRE -> ENGLISH CHANNEL (i.e., F BRE -> ENG) to contest Channel and threaten English coastal holdings. 3) A MAR -> PIE (attack Piedmont) only if Italy does not pledge no-move; otherwise A MAR -> GAS (Gascony) or support PAR->BUR as needed. 4) Keep all units active (no holds): assign supporting moves or convoys only if they are constrained by confirmed deals. I intend to verbally honor the limited non-aggression to England: I will not attack BEL or PIC this move if and only if England explicitly confirms its stand-down on Brest/Paris and gives a written promise this phase. Absent that explicit English written commitment by my order deadline, I will execute the aggressive plan (PAR->BUR and BRE->ENG) to maximize survival chances. I will not convoy English armies into my centers under any circumstance. I will communicate these conditional commitments to future self: if England confirms, change F BRE order to F BRE -> STP/NC (i.e., hold channel position) or support PAR->BUR rather than directly threatening English home centers. Note: All units will have active, non-passive orders.
goals: Survive immediate pressure by maximizing active aggression to seize nearby centers where feasible (primary targets: BUR, PIC, BEL, ENG coastal opportunities).
Secure at least one additional supply center this phase to move toward 18 SCs long-term.
Avoid being dislodged from current centers (BRE, PAR, MAR) by preemptive attacks and conditional non-aggression pacts.
Exploit diplomatic openings: convert neutral agreements into firm written commitments next phase; if commitments fail, retaliate aggressively.
storyworld_implications: The STORYWORLD_FORECAST_ARTIFACT (forecast_coalition) indicates a 60% probability that the named rival will act aggressively next phase (likely target: Austria). Negotiations referenced this forecast and used it to justify mutual stand-down proposals; it implies heightened risk of unexpected aggression and supports adopting conditional, defensive-aggressive orders now. The artifact also suggests Austria may be targeted, which could shift its focus away from western theaters if the forecast proves accurate.
forecasting_rationale: Given the forecast that aggression is more likely (0.6), I must plan for hostile moves by neighbors and avoid passive holds. Therefore I prioritize active forward moves (PAR->BUR, BRE->ENG) to seize initiative and force opponents to respond. I offer conditional non-aggression to England to reduce the risk of a two-front naval-land assault; however, because the forecast raises the chance of aggression, commitments are conditional on explicit written reciprocation. I also assume Austria may be engaged elsewhere per the forecast, so I press westward to exploit potential Austrian distraction while ensuring I do not leave southern flank undefended against Italy/Turkey opportunism.
